input.prompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:24:50.261Z</current_datetime>

My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

input.initialPrompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

data.content:
## Recommended: Thread Time (Wall Clock) Trace with PerfView

For diagnosing waits/blocks under load, collect a **Thread Time** trace. This captures both CPU and **off-CPU** time (locks, I/O, async waits) — exactly what you need.

### PerfView Command

```cmd
PerfView.exe collect /ThreadTime /NoGui /AcceptEULA ^
  /MaxCollectSec:30 /CircularMB:1024 ^
  /Providers:"Microsoft-AspNetCore-Server-Kestrel,Microsoft-Extensions-Logging" ^
  /Zip:true /Merge:true
```

### Key arguments explained

| Argument | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/ThreadTime` | Enables CPU **and** context-switch/wait tracing (CSwitchTraceData + ReadyThread) |
| `/MaxCollectSec:30` | Limits collection to 30s — reproduce the slowness during this window |
| `/CircularMB:1024` | 1 GB circular buffer; increase if events drop |
| `/Providers:...` | Adds Kestrel + logging EventSources for request correlation |
| `/Zip:true /Merge:true` | Merges & compresses into a single `.etl.zip` |

### Analysis

1. Open the `.etl.zip` in PerfView → **Thread Time (with StartStop Activities) Stacks**.
2. Group by `[ASP.NET Request]` to see per-request breakdowns.
3. Look at the **BLOCKED_TIME** node — it shows where threads waited (locks, `Task.Wait`, DB calls, etc.).

> **Tip:** If you suspect async contention specifically, add the `System.Threading.Tasks.TplEventSource` provider to capture task scheduling delays.
